name: Young-donor plasma exchange MCI pilot was feasible in a preprint but does not establish efficacy
summary: Preprint-only pilot data report feasible young-donor plasma exchange in twelve MCI patients, but uncontrolled design and preprint status keep it from changing efficacy claims.
statement: A 12-patient MCI pilot preprint reported that young-donor plasma exchange procedures were safe and feasible enough to support larger controlled testing, but it did not provide controlled efficacy evidence.
population_or_model: Patients with mild cognitive impairment and Alzheimer disease biomarker evidence
time_horizon: Pilot intervention period with short-term trajectories and registry safety follow-up planned up to four years
quantitative_note: The preprint describes 16 to 26 liters of patient plasma replaced with plasma from donors aged 18 to 24 across three dosing permutations.
caveats: The source is a preprint and has not completed peer review.
The pilot was small, uncontrolled, and disease-adjacent.
FDA cautions for young-donor plasma and donor-product variability remain central.
